title: Mean (expected value) of a discrete random variable
description: Practice calculating the mean of a discrete random variable.
topic_path: Mean (expected value) of a discrete random variable

assessment_items.question_content:
Michael is a basketball player who regularly shoots sets of $2$ free-throws. Suppose that each shot has probability $0.6$ of being made, and the results of the shots are independent.

The table below displays the probability distribution of $X$, the  number of shots that Michael makes in a set of $2$ attempts.

$X= \# \text{ of makes}$| $0$ | $1$ | $2$
- | - | - | -
$P(X)$ | $0.16$ | $0.48$ | $0.36$

**Find the expected value of the number of shots that Michael makes.**

$E(X)=$ [[☃ numeric-input 1]] makes
hints:
##Definition and formula

In the case of a discrete random variable, **expected value** or mean — denoted as $E(X)$ or $\mu_X$ — is the long-run average outcome. To find expected value, take each value, multiply it by its respective probability, and add up all the products.

$\begin{align}
E(X)=\mu_X &= x_1p_1+x_2p_2+...+x_n p_n \\\\
&= \sum x_ip_i
\end{align}$

##Calculations

$X= \# \text{ of makes}$| $\blueD{0}$ | $\greenD1$ | $\purpleD2$
- | - | - | -
$P(X)$ | $\blueE{0.16}$ | $\greenE{0.48}$ | $\purpleE{0.36}$

$\begin{align} E(X)=\mu_X &= \blueD{0}(\blueE{0.16})+\greenD{1}(\greenE{0.48})+\purpleD{2}(\purpleE{0.36}) \\\\
 &= 0+0.48+0.72 \\\\
&=1.2\end{align}$

##The answer

The expected value is $E(X)=1.2$ makes.

answers.message: Be careful. We don't round the mean (or expected value) of a discrete random variable to a whole number, since it is a long-run average.

assessment_items.question_content:
Cora is playing a game that involves flipping three coins at once. Let the random variable $H$ be the number of coins that land showing "heads". Here is the probability distribution for $H$:

$H= \# \text{ of heads}$| $0$ | $1$ | $2$ | $3$
- | - | - | - | -
$P(H)$ | $0.125$ | $0.375$ | $0.375$ | $0.125$

**Find the expected value of the number of coins that land showing "heads".**

$E(H)=$ [[☃ numeric-input 2]] "heads"
hints:
##Definition and formula

In the case of a discrete random variable, **expected value** or mean — denoted as $E(X)$ or $\mu_X$ — is the long-run average outcome. To find expected value, take each value, multiply it by its respective probability, and add up all the products.

$\begin{align}
E(X)=\mu_X &= x_1p_1+x_2p_2+...+x_n p_n \\\\
&= \sum x_ip_i
\end{align}$

##Calculations

$H= \# \text{ of heads}$| $\blueD{0}$ | $\greenD1$ | $\purpleD2$ |  $\maroonD{3}$
- | - | - | - | -
$P(H)$ | $\blueE{0.125}$ | $\greenE{0.375}$ | $\purpleE{0.375}$ | $\maroonE{0.125}$

$\begin{align} E(H)=\mu_H &= \blueD{0}(\blueE{0.125})+\greenD{1}(\greenE{0.375})+\purpleD{2}(\purpleE{0.375})+\maroonD{3}(\maroonE{0.125}) \\\\
 &= 0+0.375+0.750+0.375 \\\\
&=1.5\end{align}$

##The answer

The expected value is $E(H)=1.5$ "heads".

answers.message: Be careful. We don't round the mean (or expected value) of a discrete random variable to a whole number, since it is a long-run average.
Be careful. We don't round the mean (or expected value) of a discrete random variable to a whole number, since it is a long-run average.

assessment_items.question_content:
A couple decides to keep having children until they have a boy, at which point they will stop having children. They also agree to having a maximum of three children. The table below shows the probability distribution of $X=$ the number of children such a couple would have.

 | | | |
- | - | - | -
$X=\# \text{ of children}$| $1$ | $2$ | $3$
$P(X)$ | $\dfrac12$ | $\dfrac14$ | $\dfrac14$

**On average, how many children would such a couple have?**

$E(X)=$ [[☃ numeric-input 2]] children
hints:
##Definition and formula

In the case of a discrete random variable, **expected value** or mean — denoted as $E(X)$ or $\mu_X$ — is the long-run average outcome. To find expected value, take each value, multiply it by its respective probability, and add up all the products.

$\begin{align}
E(X)=\mu_X &= x_1p_1+x_2p_2+...+x_n p_n \\\\
&= \sum x_ip_i
\end{align}$

##Calculations

 | | | |
- | - | - | -
$X=\# \text{ of children}$| $\blueD1$ | $\greenD2$ | $\purpleD3$
$P(X)$ | $\blueE{\dfrac12}$ | $\greenE{\dfrac14}$ | $\purpleE{\dfrac14}$

$\begin{align} E(X)=\mu_X &= \blueD{1}\left(\blueE{\dfrac12}\right)+\greenD{2}\left(\greenE{\dfrac14}\right)+\purpleD{3}\left(\purpleE{\dfrac14}\right) \\\\
 &= \dfrac12+\dfrac12+\dfrac34 \\\\
&=1.75\end{align}$

##The answer

The expected value is $E(X)=1.75$ children.

answers.message: Be careful. We don't round the mean (or expected value) of a discrete random variable to a whole number, since it is a long-run average.
Be careful. We don't round the mean (or expected value) of a discrete random variable to a whole number, since it is a long-run average.

assessment_items.question_content:
Elena loves to go fishing. Each time she catches a fish, there is a $70\%$ chance that it is a northern pike and a $30\%$ chance it is a walleye. Let $X$ be the random variable that represents the number of northern pike Elena gets if she catches $2$ fish.

 | | | |
- | - | - | -
$X=\# \text{ of pike}$| $0$ | $1$ | $2$
$P(X)$ | $0.09$ | $0.42$ | $0.49$

**Find the expected value of the number of northern pike Elena catches.**

$E(X)=$ [[☃ numeric-input 3]] northern pike
hints:
##Definition and formula

In the case of a discrete random variable, **expected value** or mean — denoted as $E(X)$ or $\mu_X$ — is the long-run average outcome. To find expected value, take each value, multiply it by its respective probability, and add up all the products.

$\begin{align}
E(X)=\mu_X &= x_1p_1+x_2p_2+...+x_n p_n \\\\
&= \sum x_ip_i
\end{align}$

##Calculations

 | | | |
- | - | - | -
$X=\# \text{ of pike}$| $\blueD0$ | $\greenD1$ | $\purpleD2$
$P(X)$ | $\blueE{0.09}$ | $\greenE{0.42}$ | $\purpleE{0.49}$

$\begin{align} E(X)=\mu_X &= \blueD{0}\left(\blueE{0.09}\right)+\greenD{1}\left(\greenE{0.42}\right)+\purpleD{2}\left(\purpleE{0.49}\right) \\\\
 &= 0+0.42+0.98 \\\\
&=1.4\end{align}$

##The answer

The expected value is $E(X)=1.4$ northern pike.

answers.message: Be careful. We don't round the mean (or expected value) of a discrete random variable to a whole number, since it is a long-run average.
Be careful. We don't round the mean (or expected value) of a discrete random variable to a whole number, since it is a long-run average.

assessment_items.question_content:
Roulette is a casino game that involves players betting on where a ball will land on a spinning wheel. An American roulette wheel has $38$ numbered slots — half of the slots from $1$ to $36$ are red and the other half are black. Slots $0$ and $00$ are both green.

Suppose that a player bets $\$1$ on a single slot. If the ball lands in their slot, the player gets their initial $\$1$ back plus a payout of $\$35$. If the ball doesn't land in their slot, they lose their $\$1$ bet. Let $X=$ the player's net gain from a $\$1$ bet on a single slot. Here is the probability distribution of $X$:

 | Win | Lose | 
- | - | -
$X=\text{ net gain}$| $\$35$ | $-\$1$
$P(X)$ | $\dfrac{1}{38}$ | $\dfrac{37}{38}$

**Find the expected value of a player's net gain on a $\$1$ bet on a single slot.**  
*You may round your answer to the nearest thousandth.*

$E(X)=$ [[☃ numeric-input 3]] dollars
hints:
##Definition and formula

In the case of a discrete random variable, **expected value** or mean — denoted as $E(X)$ or $\mu_X$ — is the long-run average outcome. To find expected value, take each value, multiply it by its respective probability, and add up all the products.

$\begin{align}
E(X)=\mu_X &= x_1p_1+x_2p_2+...+x_n p_n \\\\
&= \sum x_ip_i
\end{align}$

##Calculations

 | Win | Lose | 
- | - | -
$X=\text{ net gain}$| $\blueD{\$35}$ | $\greenD{-\$1}$
$P(X)$ | $\blueE{\dfrac{1}{38}}$ | $\greenE{\dfrac{37}{38}}$

$\begin{align} E(X)=\mu_X &= \blueD{35}\left(\blueE{\dfrac{1}{38}}\right)+(\greenD{-1})\left(\greenE{\dfrac{37}{38}}\right) \\\\
 &= \dfrac{35}{38} - \dfrac{37}{38} \\\\
&= -\dfrac{2}{38} \approx -0.053
\end{align}$

##The answer

The expected value is $E(X)= -\dfrac{2}{38}$ dollars or approximately $-0.053$ dollars.

answers.message: Be careful. The mean (or expected value) of a random variable can be negative.

assessment_items.question_content:
A data scientist tracked how many cups of coffee she drank every day at work over the course of a year. She used the data to build a probability distribution where the random variable $C$ represents the number of cups of coffee she drank on a given day. Here is the distribution:

 |  |  |  |  |
:- | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-:
$C=\# \text{ of cups}$| $0$ | $1$ | $2$ | $3$
$P(C)$ | $0.05$ | $0.10$ | $0.75$ | $0.10$

**Calculate the mean of $C$.**

$\mu_C=$ [[☃ numeric-input 1]] cups
hints:
##Definition and formula:

In the case of a discrete random variable, **expected value** or mean — denoted as $E(X)$ or $\mu_X$ — is the long-run average outcome. To find expected value, take each value, multiply it by its respective probability, and add up all the products.

$\begin{align}
E(X)=\mu_X &= x_1p_1+x_2p_2+...+x_n p_n \\\\
&= \sum x_ip_i
\end{align}$

##Calculations

$C= \# \text{ of cups}$| $\blueD{0}$ | $\greenD1$ | $\purpleD2$ |  $\maroonD{3}$
- | - | - | - | -
$P(C)$ | $\blueE{0.05}$ | $\greenE{0.10}$ | $\purpleE{0.75}$ | $\maroonE{0.10}$

$\begin{align} &\phantom{=}E(C)\\\\
&=\mu_C \\\\
&= \blueD{0}(\blueE{0.15})+\greenD{1}(\greenE{0.10})+\purpleD{2}(\purpleE{0.75})+\maroonD{3}(\maroonE{0.10}) \\\\
 &= 0+0.10+1.50+0.30 \\\\
&=1.9\end{align}$

##The answer

The mean is $\mu_C=1.9$ cups.

answers.message: Be careful. We don't round the mean (or expected value) of a discrete random variable to a whole number, since it is a long-run average.

assessment_items.question_content:
A pizza shop delivers only one kind of pizza, which is sold for $\$10$, and costs the pizza shop $\$6$ to make. The pizza shop has a delivery policy that says if the pizza takes longer than half an hour to arrive, there is no charge. Experience has shown that delivery takes longer than half an hour only $10\%$ of the time.

Let the random variable $X$ be the pizza shop's profit for a randomly selected delivery order for one of these pizzas. Here is the probability distribution for $X$:

 | Late | On-time |
:- | :-: | :-: 
$X=\text{profit}$| $-\$6$ | $\$4$
$P(X)$ | $0.10$ | $0.90$

**Calculate the mean of $X$.**

$\mu_X=$ [[☃ numeric-input 1]] dollars
hints:
##Definition and formula:

In the case of a discrete random variable, **expected value** or mean — denoted as $E(X)$ or $\mu_X$ — is the long-run average outcome. To find expected value, take each value, multiply it by its respective probability, and add up all the products.

$\begin{align}
E(X)=\mu_X &= x_1p_1+x_2p_2+...+x_n p_n \\\\
&= \sum x_ip_i
\end{align}$

##Calculations

 | Late | On-time |
:- | :-: | :-: 
$X=\text{gain}$| $\blueD{-\$6}$ | $\greenD{\$4}$
$P(X)$ | $\blueE{0.10}$ | $\greenE{0.90}$

$\begin{align} E(X)=\mu_X &= \blueD{-6}(\blueE{0.10})+\greenD{4}(\greenE{0.90}) \\\\
 &= -0.6+3.6 \\\\
&=3\end{align}$

##The answer

The mean is $\mu_X=3$ dollars.

assessment_items.question_content:
Rodrigo plans to buy packs of baseball cards until he gets the card of his favorite player, but he only has enough money to buy at most three packs. Suppose that each pack has a $10\%$ chance of containing the card Rodrigo is hoping for.

The table below displays the probability distribution of $X$, the  number of packs of cards Rodrigo buys.

 |  |  |  |  
- | - | - | - 
$X=\# \text{ of packs}$| $1$ | $2$ | $3$
$P(X)$ | $0.10$ | $0.09$ | $0.81$

**Calculate the mean of $X$.**

$\mu_X=$ [[☃ numeric-input 1]] packs
hints:
##Definition and formula:

In the case of a discrete random variable, **expected value** or mean — denoted as $E(X)$ or $\mu_X$ — is the long-run average outcome. To find expected value, take each value, multiply it by its respective probability, and add up all the products.

$\begin{align}
E(X)=\mu_X &= x_1p_1+x_2p_2+...+x_n p_n \\\\
&= \sum x_ip_i
\end{align}$

##Calculations


 |  |  |  |  
- | - | - | - 
$X=\# \text{ of packs}$| $\blueD1$ | $\greenD2$ | $\purpleD3$
$P(X)$ | $\blueE{0.10}$ | $\greenE{0.09}$ | $\purpleE{0.81}$

$\begin{align}
E(X)=\mu_X &= \blueD{1}(\blueE{0.10})+\greenD{2}(\greenE{0.09})+\purpleD3(\purpleE{0.81}) \\\\
 &= 0.10+0.18+2.43 \\\\
&=2.71\end{align}$

##The answer

The mean is $\mu_X=2.71$ packs.

answers.message: Be careful. We don't round the mean (or expected value) of a discrete random variable to a whole number, since it is a long-run average.
Be careful. We don't round the mean (or expected value) of a discrete random variable to a whole number, since it is a long-run average.

assessment_items.question_content:
Eva loves to go fishing. Each time she catches a fish, there is a $70\%$ chance that it is a northern pike and a $30\%$ chance it is a walleye. Let $W$ be the random variable that represents the number of walleye Eva gets if she catches $2$ fish.

 | | | |
- | - | - | -
$W=\# \text{ of walleye}$| $0$ | $1$ | $2$
$P(W)$ | $0.49$ | $0.42$ | $0.09$

**Calculate the mean of $W$.**

$\mu_W=$ [[☃ numeric-input 4]] walleye
hints:
##Definition and formula

In the case of a discrete random variable, **expected value** or mean — denoted as $E(X)$ or $\mu_X$ — is the long-run average outcome. To find expected value, take each value, multiply it by its respective probability, and add up all the products.

$\begin{align}
E(X)=\mu_X &= x_1p_1+x_2p_2+...+x_n p_n \\\\
&= \sum x_ip_i
\end{align}$

##Calculations

 | | | |
- | - | - | -
$W=\# \text{ of walleye}$| $\blueD0$ | $\greenD1$ | $\purpleD2$
$P(W)$ | $\blueE{0.49}$ | $\greenE{0.42}$ | $\purpleE{0.09}$

$\begin{align} E(W)=\mu_W &= \blueD{0}\left(\blueE{0.49}\right)+\greenD{1}\left(\greenE{0.42}\right)+\purpleD{2}\left(\purpleE{0.09}\right) \\\\
 &= 0+0.42+0.18 \\\\
&=0.6\end{align}$

##The answer

The mean is $\mu_W=0.6$ walleye.

answers.message: Be careful. We don't round the mean (or expected value) of a discrete random variable to a whole number, since it is a long-run average.
Be careful. We don't round the mean (or expected value) of a discrete random variable to a whole number, since it is a long-run average.

assessment_items.question_content:
Roulette is a casino game that involves players betting on where a ball will land on a spinning wheel. An American roulette wheel has $38$ numbered slots — half of the slots from $1$ to $36$ are red and the other half are black. Slots $0$ and $00$ are both green.

Suppose that a player bets $\$1$ on the color red. If the ball lands in a red slot, the player gets their initial $\$1$ back plus a payout of $\$1$. If the ball doesn't land in a red slot, they lose their $\$1$ bet. Let $X=$ the player's net gain from a $\$1$ bet on a the color red. Here is the probability distribution of $X$:

 | Win | Lose | 
- | - | -
$X=\text{ net gain}$| $\$1$ | $-\$1$
$P(X)$ | $\dfrac{18}{38}$ | $\dfrac{20}{38}$

**Calculate the mean of $X$.**  
*You may round your answer to the nearest thousandth.*

$E(X)=$ [[☃ numeric-input 3]] dollars
hints:
##Definition and formula

In the case of a discrete random variable, **expected value** or mean — denoted as $E(X)$ or $\mu_X$ — is the long-run average outcome. To find expected value, take each value, multiply it by its respective probability, and add up all the products.

$\begin{align}
E(X)=\mu_X &= x_1p_1+x_2p_2+...+x_n p_n \\\\
&= \sum x_ip_i
\end{align}$

##Calculations

 | Win | Lose | 
- | - | -
$X=\text{ net gain}$| $\blueD{\$1}$ | $\greenD{-\$1}$
$P(X)$ | $\blueE{\dfrac{18}{38}}$ | $\greenE{\dfrac{20}{38}}$

$\begin{align} E(X)=\mu_X &= \blueD{1}\left(\blueE{\dfrac{18}{38}}\right)+(\greenD{-1})\left(\greenE{\dfrac{20}{38}}\right) \\\\
 &= \dfrac{18}{38} - \dfrac{20}{38} \\\\
&= -\dfrac{2}{38} \approx -0.053
\end{align}$

##The answer

The mean is $\mu_X= -\dfrac{2}{38}$ or approximately $-0.053$ dollars.

answers.message: Be careful. The mean (or expected value) of a random variable can be negative.

assessment_items.question_content:
An electronics retailer offers an optional protection plan for a mobile phone it sells. Customers can choose to buy the protection plan for $\$100$, and in case of an accident, the customer pays a $\$50$ deductible and the retailer will cover the rest of the cost of that repair. The typical cost to the retailer is $\$200$ per repair, and the plan covers a maximum of $3$ repairs.

Let $X$ be the number of repairs a randomly chosen customer uses under the protection plan, and let $F$ be the retailer's profit from one of these protection plans. Based on data from all of its customers, here are the probability distributions of $X$ and $F$:

 |  |  |  |  |
:- | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-:
$X=\# \text{ of repairs}$| $0$ | $1$ | $2$ | $3$
$F=\text{ retailer profit}$| $\$100$ | $-\$50$ | $-\$200$ | $-\$350$
**Probability** | $0.90$ | $0.07$ | $0.02$ | $0.01$

**Find the expected value of the retailer's profit per protection plan sold.**

$E(F)=$ [[☃ numeric-input 1]] dollars
hints:
##Definition and formula

In the case of a discrete random variable, **expected value** or mean — denoted as $E(X)$ or $\mu_X$ — is the long-run average outcome. To find expected value, take each value, multiply it by its respective probability, and add up all the products.

$\begin{align}
E(X)=\mu_X &= x_1p_1+x_2p_2+...+x_n p_n \\\\
&= \sum x_ip_i
\end{align}$

##Calculations

We're only concerned about the random variable $F$ in this case: 

|  |  |  |  |
:- | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-:
$X=\# \text{ of repairs}$| $0$ | $1$ | $2$ | $3$
$F=\text{ retailer profit}$| $\blueD{\$100}$ | $\greenD{-\$50}$ | $\purpleD{-\$200}$ | $\maroonD{-\$350}$
**Probability** | $\blueE{0.90}$ | $\greenE{0.07}$ | $\purpleE{0.02}$ | $\maroonE{0.01}$

$\begin{align} &\phantom{=}E(F)\\\\
&=\mu_F \\\\
&= \blueD{100}(\blueE{0.90})+(\greenD{-50})(\greenE{0.07})+(\purpleD{-200})(\purpleE{0.02})+(\maroonD{-350})(\maroonE{0.01}) \\\\
 &= 90-3.5-4-3.5 \\\\
&=79\end{align}$

##The answer

The expected value is $E(F)=79$ dollars.

assessment_items.question_content:
Kelsie works at a bicycle shop as a salesperson. She records the number of bicycles she sells on a daily basis. Here is the probability distribution of $B=$ the number of bicycles Kelsie sells on a randomly selected day, and $T=$ the time she spends filling out daily sales reports.

 |  |  |  |  |
:- | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-:
$B=\# \text{ of bicycles sold}$| $0$ | $1$ | $2$ | $3$
$T=\text{time (minutes)}$| $0$ | $10$ | $20$ | $30$
**Probability** | $0.30$ | $0.50$ | $0.15$ | $0.05$

**Find the expected value of the amount of time Kelsie spends filling out daily sales reports.**

$E(T)=$ [[☃ numeric-input 2]] minutes
hints:
##Definition and formula:

In the case of a discrete random variable, **expected value** or mean — denoted as $E(X)$ or $\mu_X$ — is the long-run average outcome. To find expected value, take each value, multiply it by its respective probability, and add up all the products.

$\begin{align}
E(X)=\mu_X &= x_1p_1+x_2p_2+...+x_n p_n \\\\
&= \sum x_ip_i
\end{align}$

##Calculations

We're only concerned about the random variable $T$ in this case:

 |  |  |  |  |
:- | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-:
$B=\# \text{ of bicycles sold}$| $0$ | $1$ | $2$ | $3$
$T=\text{time (minutes)}$| $\blueD{0}$ | $\greenD{10}$ | $\purpleD{20}$ | $\maroonD{30}$
**Probability** | $\blueE{0.30}$ | $\greenE{0.50}$ | $\purpleE{0.15}$ | $\maroonE{0.05}$

$\begin{align} &\phantom{=}E(T)\\\\
&=\mu_T \\\\
&= \blueD{0}(\blueE{0.30})+\greenD{10}(\greenE{0.50})+\purpleD{20}(\purpleE{0.15})+\maroonD{30}(\maroonE{0.05}) \\\\
 &= 0+5+3+1.5 \\\\
&=9.5\end{align}$

##The answer

The expected value is $E(T)=9.5$ minutes.

answers.message: Be careful. We don't round the mean (or expected value) of a random variable to a whole number, since it is a long-run average.
Be careful. We don't round the mean (or expected value) of a discrete random variable to a whole number, since it is a long-run average.

assessment_items.question_content:
Jordan wants to play a basketball game at a carnival. The game costs the player $\$5$ to play, and the player gets to take two long-distance shots. If they miss both shots, they get nothing. If they make one shot, they get their $\$5$ back. If they make both shots, they get $\$10$ back. Jordan has a $40\%$ chance of making this type of shot.

 Here is the probability distribution of $X=$ the number of shots Jordan makes in a randomly selected game, and $M=$ the amount of money Jordan gains from playing the game.

 |  |  |  | 
:- | :-: | :-: | :-:
$X=\# \text{ of shots made}$| $0$ | $1$ | $2$
$M=\text{money gained}$| $-\$5$ | $\$0$ | $\$5$
**Probability** | $0.36$ | $0.48$ | $0.16$

**Find the expected value of the amount of money Jordan gains from playing this game.**

$E(M)=$ [[☃ numeric-input 2]] dollars
hints:
##Definition and formula:

In the case of a discrete random variable, **expected value** or mean — denoted as $E(X)$ or $\mu_X$ — is the long-run average outcome. To find expected value, take each value, multiply it by its respective probability, and add up all the products.

$\begin{align}
E(X)=\mu_X &= x_1p_1+x_2p_2+...+x_n p_n \\\\
&= \sum x_ip_i
\end{align}$

##Calculations

We're only concerned about the random variable $M$ in this case:

 |  |  |  | 
:- | :-: | :-: | :-:
$X=\# \text{ of shots made}$| $0$ | $1$ | $2$
$M=\text{money gained}$| $\blueD{-\$5}$ | $\greenD{\$0}$ | $\purpleD{\$5}$
**Probability** | $\blueE{0.36}$ | $\greenE{0.48}$ | $\purpleE{0.16}$

$\begin{align} E(M)=\mu_M &= \blueD{-5}(\blueE{0.36})+\greenD{0}(\greenE{0.48})+\purpleD{5}(\purpleE{0.16}) \\\\
 &= -1.8+0+0.8 \\\\
&=-1\end{align}$

##The answer

The expected value is $E(M)=-1$ dollar.

answers.message: Be careful. The mean (or expected value) of a random variable can be negative.

assessment_items.question_content:
A construction company is considering submitting bids for two contracts. It will cost the company $\$10{,}000$ to prepare and submit the bids, and if won, each bid would produce $\$50{,}000$ of income to the company. The company estimates that it has a $10\%$ chance of winning any given bid.

 Here is the probability distribution of $X=$ the number of bids the company wins, and $M=$ the amount of money the company profits from the bids.

 |  |  |  | 
:- | :-: | :-: | :-:
$X=\# \text{ of bids won}$| $0$ | $1$ | $2$
$M=\text{profit}$| $-\$10{,}000$ | $\$40{,}000$ | $\$90{,}000$
**Probability** | $0.81$ | $0.18$ | $0.01$

**Find the expected value of the number of bids won.**

$E(X)=$ [[☃ numeric-input 4]] bids
hints:
##Definition and formula:

In the case of a discrete random variable, **expected value** or mean — denoted as $E(X)$ or $\mu_X$ — is the long-run average outcome. To find expected value, take each value, multiply it by its respective probability, and add up all the products.

$\begin{align}
E(X)=\mu_X &= x_1p_1+x_2p_2+...+x_n p_n \\\\
&= \sum x_ip_i
\end{align}$

##Calculations

We're only concerned about the random variable $X$ in this case:

 |  |  |  | 
:- | :-: | :-: | :-:
$X=\# \text{ of bids won}$| $\blueD0$ | $\greenD1$ | $\purpleD2$
$M=\text{profit}$| $-\$10{,}000$ | $\$40{,}000$ | $\$90{,}000$
**Probability** | $\blueE{0.81}$ | $\greenE{0.18}$ | $\purpleE{0.01}$

$\begin{align} E(X)=\mu_X &= \blueD{0}(\blueE{0.81})+\greenD{1}(\greenE{0.18})+\purpleD{2}(\purpleE{0.01}) \\\\
 &= 0+0.18+0.02 \\\\
&=0.2\end{align}$

##The answer

The expected value is $E(X)=0.2$ bids.

answers.message: Be careful. We don't round the mean (or expected value) of a discrete random variable to a whole number, since it is a long-run average.

assessment_items.question_content:
An electronics retailer offers an optional protection plan for a mobile phone it sells. Customers can choose to buy the protection plan for $\$100$, and in case of an accident, the customer pays a $\$50$ deductible and the retailer will cover the rest of the cost of that repair. The typical cost to the retailer is $\$200$ per repair, and the plan covers a maximum of $3$ repairs.

Let $X$ be the number of repairs a randomly chosen customer uses under the protection plan, and let $F$ be the retailer's profit from one of these protection plans. Based on data from all of its customers, here are the probability distributions of $X$ and $F$:

 |  |  |  |  |
:- | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-:
$X=\# \text{ of repairs}$| $0$ | $1$ | $2$ | $3$
$F=\text{ retailer profit}$| $\$100$ | $-\$50$ | $-\$200$ | $-\$350$
**Probability** | $0.90$ | $0.07$ | $0.02$ | $0.01$

**Calculate the mean of $X$.**

$\mu_X=$ [[☃ numeric-input 1]] repairs
hints:
##Definition and formula

In the case of a discrete random variable, **expected value** or mean — denoted as $E(X)$ or $\mu_X$ — is the long-run average outcome. To find expected value, take each value, multiply it by its respective probability, and add up all the products.

$\begin{align}
E(X)=\mu_X &= x_1p_1+x_2p_2+...+x_n p_n \\\\
&= \sum x_ip_i
\end{align}$

##Calculations

We're only concerned about the random variable $X$ in this case:

 |  |  |  |  |
:- | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-:
$X=\# \text{ of repairs}$| $\blueD0$ | $\greenD1$ | $\purpleD2$ | $\maroonD3$
$F=\text{ retailer profit}$| $\$100$ | $-\$50$ | $-\$200$ | $-\$350$
**Probability** | $\blueE{0.90}$ | $\greenE{0.07}$ | $\purpleE{0.02}$ | $\maroonE{0.01}$

$\begin{align} E(X)=\mu_X &= \blueD{0}(\blueE{0.90})+\greenD{1}(\greenE{0.07})+\purpleD{2}(\purpleE{0.02})+\maroonD{3}(\maroonE{0.01}) \\\\
 &= 0+0.07+0.04+0.03 \\\\
&=0.14\end{align}$

##The answer

The mean is $\mu_X=0.14$ repairs.

answers.message: Careful, we don't round the mean (or expected value) of a discrete random variable to a whole number, since it is a long-run average.

assessment_items.question_content:
Kelsie works at a bicycle shop as a salesperson. She records the number of bicycles she sells on a daily basis. Here is the probability distribution of $B=$ the number of bicycles Kelsie sells on a randomly selected day, and $T=$ the time she spends filling out daily sales reports.

 |  |  |  |  |
:- | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-:
$B=\# \text{ of bicycles sold}$| $0$ | $1$ | $2$ | $3$
$T=\text{time (minutes)}$| $10$ | $20$ | $30$ | $40$
**Probability** | $0.30$ | $0.50$ | $0.15$ | $0.05$

**Calculate the mean of $B$.**

$\mu_B=$ [[☃ numeric-input 1]] bicycles
hints:
##Definition and formula:

In the case of a discrete random variable, **expected value** or mean — denoted as $E(X)$ or $\mu_X$ — is the long-run average outcome. To find expected value, take each value, multiply it by its respective probability, and add up all the products.

$\begin{align}
E(X)=\mu_X &= x_1p_1+x_2p_2+...+x_n p_n \\\\
&= \sum x_ip_i
\end{align}$

##Calculations

We're only concerned about the random variable $B$ in this case:

 |  |  |  |  |
:- | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-:
$B=\# \text{ of bicycles sold}$| $\blueD0$ | $\greenD1$ | $\purpleD2$ | $\maroonD3$
$T=\text{time (minutes)}$| $10$ | $20$ | $30$ | $40$
**Probability** | $\blueE{0.30}$ | $\greenE{0.50}$ | $\purpleE{0.15}$ | $\maroonE{0.05}$

$\begin{align} E(B)=\mu_B &= \blueD{0}(\blueE{0.30})+\greenD{1}(\greenE{0.50})+\purpleD{2}(\purpleE{0.15})+\maroonD{3}(\maroonE{0.05}) \\\\
 &= 0+0.50+0.30+0.15 \\\\

##The answer

The mean is $\mu_B=0.95$ bicycles.

answers.message: Careful, we don't round the mean (or expected value) of a discrete random variable to a whole number, since it is a long-run average.

assessment_items.question_content:
A construction company is considering submitting bids for two contracts. It will cost the company $\$10{,}000$ to prepare and submit the bids, and if won, each bid would produce $\$50{,}000$ of income to the company. The company estimates that it has a $10\%$ chance of winning any given bid.

 Here is the probability distribution of $X=$ the number of bids the company wins, and $M=$ the amount of money the company profits from the bids.

 |  |  |  | 
:- | :-: | :-: | :-:
$X=\# \text{ of bids won}$| $0$ | $1$ | $2$
$M=\text{profit}$| $-\$10{,}000$ | $\$40{,}000$ | $\$90{,}000$
**Probability** | $0.81$ | $0.18$ | $0.01$

**Calculate the mean of $M$.**

$\mu_M=$ [[☃ numeric-input 3]] dollars
hints:
##Definition and formula:

In the case of a discrete random variable, **expected value** or mean — denoted as $E(X)$ or $\mu_X$ — is the long-run average outcome. To find expected value, take each value, multiply it by its respective probability, and add up all the products.

$\begin{align}
E(X)=\mu_X &= x_1p_1+x_2p_2+...+x_n p_n \\\\
&= \sum x_ip_i
\end{align}$

##Calculations

We're only concerned about the random variable $M$ in this case:

 |  |  |  | 
:- | :-: | :-: | :-:
$X=\# \text{ of bids won}$| $0$ | $1$ | $2$
$M=\text{profit}$| $\blueD{-\$10{,}000}$ | $\greenD{\$40{,}000}$ | $\purpleD{\$90{,}000}$
**Probability** | $\blueE{0.81}$ | $\greenE{0.18}$ | $\purpleE{0.01}$

$\begin{align} &\phantom{=}E(M)\\\\
&=\mu_M\\\\
 &= \blueD{-\$10{,}000}(\blueE{0.81})+\greenD{\$40{,}000}(\greenE{0.18})+\purpleD{\$90{,}000}(\purpleE{0.01}) \\\\
 &= -8{,}100+7{,}200+900 \\\\
&=0\end{align}$

##The answer

The mean is $\mu_M=0$ dollars.

assessment_items.question_content:
Jordan wants to play a basketball game at a carnival. The game costs the player $\$5$ to play, and the player gets to take two long-distance shots. If they miss both shots, they get nothing. If they make one shot, they get their $\$5$ back. If they make both shots, they get $\$10$ back. Jordan has a $40\%$ chance of making this type of shot.

 Here is the probability distribution of $X=$ the number of shots Jordan makes in a randomly selected game, and $M=$ the amount of money Jordan gains from playing the game.

 |  |  |  | 
:- | :-: | :-: | :-:
$X=\# \text{ of shots made}$| $0$ | $1$ | $2$
$M=\text{money gained}$| $-\$5$ | $\$0$ | $\$5$
**Probability** | $0.36$ | $0.48$ | $0.16$

**Calculate the mean of $X$.**

$\mu_X=$ [[☃ numeric-input 3]] shots made
hints:
##Definition and formula:

In the case of a discrete random variable, **expected value** or mean — denoted as $E(X)$ or $\mu_X$ — is the long-run average outcome. To find expected value, take each value, multiply it by its respective probability, and add up all the products.

$\begin{align}
E(X)=\mu_X &= x_1p_1+x_2p_2+...+x_n p_n \\\\
&= \sum x_ip_i
\end{align}$

##Calculations

We're only concerned about the random variable $X$ in this case:

 |  |  |  | 
:- | :-: | :-: | :-:
$X=\# \text{ of shots made}$| $\blueD0$ | $\greenD1$ | $\purpleD2$
$M=\text{money gained}$| $-\$5$ | $\$0$ | $\$5$
**Probability** | $\blueE{0.36}$ | $\greenE{0.48}$ | $\purpleE{0.16}$

$\begin{align} E(X)=\mu_X &= \blueD{0}(\blueE{0.36})+\greenD{1}(\greenE{0.48})+\purpleD{2}(\purpleE{0.16}) \\\\
 &= 0+0.48+0.32 \\\\
&=0.8\end{align}$

##The answer

The mean is $\mu_X=0.8$ shots made.

answers.message: Careful, we don't round the mean (or expected value) of a discrete random variable to the nearest whole number, since it is a long-run average.
